metadata.author: Hafize Sanlı
metadata.description: Prompts used to generate GraphWalker model-based test cases with different coverage strategies (REC, QEC, VC) for three different models
metadata.research_goal: Evaluate and compare the ability of different LLMs to generate valid test suites with various coverage strategies (Random Edge Coverage, Quick Random Edge Coverage, Random Vertex Coverage) for finite state machine models

REC.name: Random Edge Coverage
REC.description: Generates test paths using random(edge_coverage(100)) algorithm

QEC.name: Quick Random Edge Coverage
QEC.description: Generates test paths using quick_random(edge_coverage(100)) algorithm

VC.name: Vertex Coverage
VC.description: Generates test paths using random(vertex_coverage(100)) algorithm

TLC.description: A simple finite state machine representing a traffic light controller with states q0-q8 and start vertex

Risc-V.description: A RISC-V CPU model with various instruction sets and pipelines

Parabank.description: Multi-module banking application with Login, Register, AccountOverview modules

Testinium.description: Enterprise test management platform with multiple modules: Login, Dashboard, Reports, Projects, Scenarios, Suites

output_format.description: Space-separated JSON objects containing currentElementName field
requirements: Each element must be a JSON object with 'currentElementName' field
Objects must be separated by single spaces
Elements include both vertices (states) and edges (transitions)
The sequence must follow valid paths in the model
Must achieve the target coverage (edge or vertex depending on strategy)

REC.template:
You are an expert in Software Testing, specialized in Model-Based Testing, and an expert in the Graphwalker tool. Graphwalker models TLC.json, Testinium.json, Parabank.json and Risc-V.json are provided to you. You are tasked with generating graphwalker model-based test cases for these models and save them in provided txt files. You will create a test suite having 100% random edge coverage. Please create the test suite in raw form, as shown in the example below:
{"currentElementName":"start"} {"currentElementName":"s"} {"currentElementName":"q0"} {"currentElementName":"h"} {"currentElementName":"q8"}

variations.per_model_detailed:
You are an expert in Software Testing, specialized in Model-Based Testing, and an expert in the Graphwalker tool. Graphwalker model {MODEL_NAME}.json is provided to you. You are tasked with generating graphwalker model-based test cases for this model. You will create a test suite having 100% random edge coverage. Please create the test suite in raw form, as shown in the example below: {"currentElementName":"start"} {"currentElementName":"s"} {"currentElementName":"q0"} {"currentElementName":"h"} {"currentElementName":"q8"}...
Don't create python script, just give me the test suite in a txt file and save it in {LLM_FOLDER} folder.
variations.explicit_no_copy:
You are an expert in Software Testing, specialized in Model-Based Testing, and an expert in the Graphwalker tool. Graphwalker models TLC.json, Testinium.json, Parabank.json and Risc-V.json are provided to you. You are tasked with generating graphwalker model-based test cases for these models and save them in provided txt files ({LLM_FOLDER}/Parabank.txt, {LLM_FOLDER}/Testinium.txt, {LLM_FOLDER}/TLC.txt). Don't copy other folder's txt files, create yourself. Don't create a python script for that. Just do what I said. You will create a test suite having 100% random edge coverage. Please create the test suite in raw form, as shown in the example below:
{"currentElementName":"start"} {"currentElementName":"s"} {"currentElementName":"q0"} {"currentElementName":"h"} {"currentElementName":"q8"}

QEC.template:
You are an expert in Software Testing, specialized in Model-Based Testing, and an expert in the Graphwalker tool. Graphwalker models TLC.json, Testinium.json, Parabank.json and Risc-V.json are provided to you. You are tasked with generating graphwalker model-based test cases for these models and save them in provided txt files. You will create a test suite having 100% quick_random edge coverage. Please create the test suite in raw form, as shown in the example below:
{"currentElementName":"start"} {"currentElementName":"s"} {"currentElementName":"q0"} {"currentElementName":"h"} {"currentElementName":"q8"}
QEC.note: Use 'quick_random' (with underscore) instead of 'quick random' (with space) - the underscore version works better with most LLMs

attempts.prompt:
You are an expert in Software Testing, specialized in Model-Based Testing, and an expert in the Graphwalker tool. Graphwalker models TLC.json, Testinium.json and Parabank.json are provided to you. You are tasked with generating graphwalker model-based test cases for these models and save them in provided txt files (Gemini-2.5-Pro/Parabank.txt, Gemini-2.5-Pro/Testinium.txt, Gemini-2.5-Pro/TLC.txt). Don't copy other folder's txt files or run a graphwalker command, create yourself. You will create a test suite having 100% random edge coverage. Please create the test suite in raw form, as shown in the example below:
{"currentElementName":"start"} {"currentElementName":"s"} {"currentElementName":"q0"} {"currentElementName":"h"} {"currentElementName":"q8"}
attempts.notes: Generated all three models successfully after clarification

attempts.notes: Told added the test suites to the file but file was empty (3 times)

key_elements: Explicitly state 'random edge coverage' or 'vertex coverage' or 'quick_random edge coverage'
Use exact GraphWalker terminology (underscore in 'quick_random', not space)
Add 'Don't copy other folders' and 'Don't create Python script' warnings
Request all three models at once (works better for most LLMs)
successful_prompts.most_effective_template:
You are an expert in Software Testing, specialized in Model-Based Testing, and an expert in the Graphwalker tool. Graphwalker models TLC.json, Testinium.json and Parabank.json are provided to you. You are tasked with generating graphwalker model-based test cases for these models and save them in provided txt files. You will create a test suite having 100% {STRATEGY} coverage. Please create the test suite in raw form, as shown in the example below:
{"currentElementName":"start"} {"currentElementName":"s"} {"currentElementName":"q0"} {"currentElementName":"h"} {"currentElementName":"q8"}

copying_files.solution: Add explicit instruction: 'Don't copy other folder's txt files, create yourself'

python_scripts.solution: Add explicit instruction: 'Don't create a python script for that. Just do what I said'

chat_output.solution: Some LLMs prefer to output to chat and ask user to paste. May need explicit file writing instruction

misunderstanding_task.solution: Add: 'Don't run a graphwalker command, create yourself'

no_response.solution: May need to break down into smaller requests or try different prompt variations

length_limit.solution: Response may hit token limit. Use 'quick_random' for shorter paths or request models separately

wrong_coverage.solution: Use exact GraphWalker terminology with underscores: 'quick_random' not 'quick random'

step_1.description: Decide which coverage strategy to test

step_2.description: Create a directory with strategy prefix for each LLM being tested

step_3.description: Give the LLM access to the GraphWalker JSON model files

step_4.description: Use the prompt template from 'prompt_templates' section, replacing {STRATEGY} with your chosen strategy

step_5.description: Ensure the generated test suites are in raw format with proper JSON structure
step_5.validation: Each line should contain space-separated {"currentElementName":"name"} objects

step_6.description: Execute all test suites for the LLM and collect results

step_7.description: Generate comparison reports across all tested LLMs for same strategy

step_8.description: Document the successful prompts and any issues encountered in this file

edge_coverage.description: Percentage of edges covered in the model
edge_coverage.target_VC: May be lower, depends on model structure

vertex_coverage.description: Percentage of vertices visited in the model

success_rate.description: Percentage of test suites that passed validation

success_determination.rationale: Different strategies optimize for different coverage types
success_determination.note: REC/QEC tests should achieve high edge coverage; VC tests should achieve high vertex coverage

path_validity.description: Whether all transitions in the test suite are valid according to the model

format_compliance.description: Whether the output matches the required raw format

path_length.description: Number of steps in the test path
path_length.importance: QEC may produce longer paths than REC for same coverage

research_notes.hypothesis: Modern LLMs can effectively generate valid model-based test cases with different coverage strategies for finite state machine models
expected_outcomes: REC and QEC should achieve similar edge coverage, but QEC may have longer paths
VC should achieve high vertex coverage but may have lower edge coverage
All LLMs should achieve >90% target coverage on simple TLC model
Complex models (Parabank, Testinium) will reveal capability differences
Format compliance may vary across LLMs
Larger context window models may perform better on complex models
findings: Claude models (Opus, Sonnet) are most reliable for all strategies
GPT-5.2 performs well with explicit file path instructions
GPT-5.1 tends to output to chat instead of creating files
Gemini models often misunderstand instructions or copy files
Gemini-2.5-Pro needs explicit 'don't run graphwalker command' instruction
Some LLMs hit response length limits on complex models
